onboardingChooser.heading: Start a project
onboardingChooser.description: {brandName} develops a project from its canonical spec. Open an existing repo or start from scratch, then bring the spec in — import docs you already have, or build it from nothing by talking to Vivi.

openProject.title: Open a project
openProject.description: Pick a repository that already carries a .vivicy/. {brandName} runs the method against it and adds only development output.

scaffold.title: Start from scratch
scaffold.description: Give a new empty folder and a name. {brandName} scaffolds a lean skeleton — you write the canonical spec afterwards.

importDocs.title: Import your docs
importDocs.description: Drop existing spec files, a folder, or a .zip. {brandName} checks them for drift, then places the canonical, spikes, and map for you.

buildWithVivi.title: Build the spec with Vivi
buildWithVivi.description: No docs yet? Talk to Vivi. She grills you until your idea is a rigorous canonical spec and writes it into .vivicy/ for you.

openProjectDialog.title: Open project
openProjectDialog.description: Choose the local repository {brandName} should develop. Navigate to a folder and select it, or paste an absolute path.

toast.selectedNoSpec: {root} (no docs/ — extract has no spec to read yet)

scaffoldDialog.title: Start from scratch
scaffoldDialog.description: {brandName} scaffolds a new project with the full development method — you write the canonical product spec afterwards.

toast.successDescription: {root} — write your canonical docs, then extract.

importDocsDialog.title: Import your docs
importDocsDialog.description: Drop files, a folder, or a .zip. {brandName} checks the corpus for drift or contradictions before placing anything — nothing lands in .vivicy/ until the check is green.

dropzone.prompt: Drag files, a folder, or a .zip here

staged.count: Staged {count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}}

verified.collisionsIntro: Apply refused — these destinations already exist and were not touched:

placed.count: Placed {count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}}

toast.folderReadErrorTitle: Could not read the dropped folder
toast.folderReadErrorDescription: This browser can't read folder contents from a drop. Use “Choose folder” instead.
